title: Raj edu department, UNICEF launch heatwave teaching module for Classes 6–12 in govt schools - The Times of India
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
Jaipur: The Rajasthan education dept, in partnership with UNICEF, has developed a 40-minute lesson plan on protection against heatwaves or extreme heat for govt school students of classes 6 to 12 to build awareness of climate change and extreme heat risks.
“Children are particularly vulnerable to extreme heat. Their bodies are less efficient at regulating temperature than adults, and they are directly exposed to heat while commuting to and from school. It is therefore essential to educate them about heatwave prevention measures and the science behind rising temperatures so they can remain alert and aware of climate change,” a senior official said.
The module, accessed by
TOI, focuses on training and sensitising students on heatwave impacts, protective measures, water conservation and environmental protection. Titled ‘Rajasthan Mein Bhishan Garmi’ (extreme heat in Rajasthan), the module also covers practical themes such as water budgeting on school campuses and nearby areas, linking activities like measuring water use to mathematics concepts including volume.
As part of the module, students are also being informed about the signs of heat-related illnesses that happen when the temperature crosses 40°C, like heat cramps (sudden painful muscle spasms due to loss of salt and water through sweat), heat exhaustion (excessive sweating, weakness, cold skin, rapid pulse, dizziness, nausea) and heatstroke (no sweating, skin hot and dry, temperature above 40°C, confusion, unconsciousness), which is a medical emergency.
To introduce students to the module, teachers have been asked to start conversations around how they feel at around 2pm outside the classroom, if anyone in the family experienced dizziness, fatigue or headaches from the heat, or if it seems hotter this year than in previous years and the reason behind it.
Further, to create a heat memory map, students are being asked to close their eyes and imagine the hottest day ever and how did their body feel that day. Along with such exercises, teachers have been asked to generate curiosity among students about why the temperatures are rising so much.
“A pilot testing of the module was done before the schools closed for summer vacations, which was the peak heat time in the state. The module is essentially about making children understand the concept of climate change, heatwave, what it feels like, how they can address it,” an official from the education dept said.

title: As heatwave raises fire risk, Centre issues electrical safety alert after fatal short-circuit incidents - The Times of India
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
MUMBAI: "Replace damaged wires, loose plugs and overheating sockets immediately. Install smoke detectors near electrical panels. Keep a suitable fire extinguisher accessible. Never attempt electrical repairs yourself unless qualified. And keep a close watch on frequent tripping of breakers, burning smell near switchboards, sparking sockets, flickering lights and unusually hot plugs, switches or electrical panels."
Against the backdrop of a series of deadly fire incidents linked to suspected electrical short circuits, the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) has issued a nationwide safety advisory urging electricity consumers, housing societies, hospitals, schools, malls and utilities to urgently inspect and maintain their electrical systems before peak summer temperatures place further stress on power infrastructure.
The advisory comes after fatal fires in a high-rise building in Delhi's Palam area, a residence in Indore and a medical college hospital in Cuttack, where electrical faults were cited among the probable causes. With the
India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasting above-normal heatwave conditions and temperatures crossing 45 degree celcius in several regions, the CEA warned that transformers, cables, switchgear and internal wiring systems face increased loading and overheating risks.
For Mumbai's millions of apartment dwellers, office-goers and commercial establishments, the warning carries particular significance. Electrical experts note that air-conditioners, refrigerators, pumps and other appliances running continuously during summer can overload ageing wiring and distribution systems.
The CEA's advisory highlights several precautions that consumers can implement immediately to reduce fire risks. Among the most important recommendations include; ensure that electrical repair work is carried out only by licensed electricians or qualified personnel, install a Residual Current Device (RCD) or Earth Leakage Circuit Breaker (ELCB) with a tripping current not exceeding 30 milliamperes in homes to prevent electrocution and detect leakage currents, verify that earthing systems are functioning properly, particularly in older buildings and housing societies, install and maintain adequate fire extinguishers and fire alarm systems for early detection and response, and, avoid unauthorised electrical modifications, illegal tapping or overloading circuits with multiple high-power appliances.
The advisory places special emphasis on high-rise buildings, hospitals, schools, malls, hotels, cinema halls, banks and other locations with heavy public footfall. It recommends continuous monitoring of electrical systems through modern technologies capable of detecting early warning signs such as arcing, short circuits, earth leakage, over-current conditions, voltage fluctuations and loss of neutral connections before they escalate into fires.
For resident welfare associations and cooperative housing societies, the CEA has stressed periodic inspection and testing of electrical installations, proper earthing of switchgear and panels, maintenance of firefighting equipment, and ensuring that only trained personnel handle electrical operations.
Electrical safety experts say one of the most overlooked risks in Mumbai's ageing residential stock is the mismatch between modern electricity demand and decades-old wiring systems. Many buildings designed before widespread use of air-conditioners now face significantly higher loads during summer, increasing the likelihood of overheating cables and distribution boards.
The CEA has chosen "Today's Awareness, Tomorrow's Prevention – Let Us All Unite to Prevent Fire Hazards from Electrical Short Circuits" as the theme for Electrical Safety Week 2026 beginning June 26, aiming to spread awareness among consumers and utilities alike.
Officials have urged stakeholders to treat electrical safety as a public-health issue rather than merely a technical matter, warning that timely inspections and preventive maintenance could save lives, prevent property losses and reduce the risk of catastrophic fires during the ongoing summer season.
Quick Checklist For Households
Do this this week:- Get your home's wiring inspected if the building is over 15 years old.
- Test earthing and circuit breakers.
- Avoid using multiple ACs, heaters and heavy appliances on overloaded extension boards.
- Replace damaged wires, loose plugs and overheating sockets immediately.
- Never attempt electrical repairs yourself unless qualified.
Warning signs not to ignore:- Frequent tripping of breakers.
- Unusually hot plugs, switches or electrical panels.
Experts say these seemingly minor symptoms often precede electrical fires and should trigger immediate inspection.

title: ‘Intense heat’: Five students from same school fall ill - MillenniumPost
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
‘Intense heat’: Five students from same school fall ill
Jalpaiguri: Five students of Gadheyarkuthi High School in Dhupguri fell ill during school hours on Friday, reportedly owing to “intense heat”, and were admitted to Dhupguri Sub-Divisional Hospital for treatment.
According to school sources, the students suddenly complained of physical discomfort while attending classes shortly after having their mid-day meal.
Teachers immediately took them to a nearby health centre, where they received preliminary treatment. From there, the students were subsequently transported to the hospital in vehicles arranged by the school authorities.
After being admitted to the emergency department, doctors kept them under observation. Their condition is stated to be stable.
School authorities said the exact reason behind the students falling ill simultaneously has not yet been confirmed.
Acting Headmaster Hiranmoy Roy, said: “The students suddenly fell ill during class hours.
We immediately took them to the nearby health centre, from where they were referred to the Dhupguri Sub-Divisional Hospital. They are currently undergoing treatment.”

title: Health Director Urges Residents to Watch for Heat Illnesses as Summer Temperatures Rise - TCHDailyNews
search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
SHAWANO/MENOMINEE COUNTIES, WI – With temperatures expected to climb over the next several days, Shawano-Menominee Counties Health Director Nick Mau is encouraging residents to take precautions and recognize the warning signs of heat-related illnesses.
Mau said there are three primary heat illnesses people should be aware of: heat stroke, heat exhaustion and heat cramps.
“Heat-related illness, there’s a couple different things. You can have a heat stroke, heat exhaustion, you can have heat cramps,” Mau said.
According to Mau, heat stroke is the most serious condition and can quickly become life-threatening. Symptoms include a body temperature of 103 degrees or higher, hot red skin, a rapid pulse, headache, dizziness, nausea, confusion and even loss of consciousness.
“Heat stroke is something that you don’t want to mess with and call 911,” Mau said. “Better safe than sorry.”
Mau explained that heat exhaustion is often marked by heavy sweating, cool and clammy skin, weakness, nausea, dizziness and muscle cramps. Heat cramps are typically caused by intense exercise or physical labor in hot conditions and can lead to painful muscle spasms and excessive sweating.
For anyone experiencing heat-related symptoms, Mau recommends immediately stopping activity, moving to a cooler location and rehydrating. Those suffering from heat exhaustion or heat cramps should sip water or sports drinks containing electrolytes and use cool washcloths to lower body temperature. In cases of suspected heat stroke, he advises calling 911, moving the person into shade or air conditioning, applying cold compresses and avoiding giving the person anything to drink while waiting for emergency responders.
With youth sports camps, recreation programs and outdoor activities filling summer schedules, Mau is also reminding coaches and parents to be proactive.
“Make rest and those short breaks intentional,” Mau said. “Make sure that all families are told to have plenty of water or sports drinks brought with their kiddos.”
He added that athletes and children should drink more fluids than they think they need because heat-related illnesses can develop quickly.
“You can’t overdo the amount of water that you have on supply,” Mau said. “Drink more than you would even think you would need because those heat illnesses can really creep up on you quickly.”
Health officials encourage residents to stay hydrated, take frequent breaks in shaded areas and pay close attention to early signs of heat stress as the summer heat settles into the region.

title: आम उत्पादकों के लिए अलर्ट: 44 डिग्री तक पहुँचेगा तापमान, बढ़ेगा कीटों का ख़तरा, अपनाएं ये ज़रूरी सावधानियाँ - Gaon Connection
search_term: ("पारा चढ़ा" OR "पारा पहुंचा" OR तापमान OR "तापमान बढ़ा" OR "रिकॉर्ड तोड़ गर्मी" OR "डिग्री सेल्सियस" OR "पारा उबला" OR "अधिकतम तापमान" OR "तापमान 40 पार" OR "तापमान 45 पार" OR "तापमान बढ़ने की संभावना") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
Mango Harvesting Alert: आम उत्पादक हो जाएं सतर्क, 44 डिग्री तक पहुँचेगा तापमान, बढ़ेगा कीटों का ख़तरा, अपनाएं ये ज़रूरी सावधानियाँ
उत्तर प्रदेश में मानसून की शुरुआत और बढ़ते तापमान के बीच यूपीसीएआर ने आम उत्पादकों के लिए विशेष एडवाइजरी जारी की है। वैज्ञानिकों के अनुसार अगले दो सप्ताह तक कई क्षेत्रों में तापमान 40 से 44 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुँच सकता है, जिससे फलमक्खी, शोल्डर ब्राउनिंग और कटर कीट का खतरा बढ़ सकता है। ऐसे में बागवानों को तुड़ाई, कीट-रोग प्रबंधन और बागों की देखभाल में विशेष सावधानी बरतने की सलाह दी गई है, ताकि फलों की गुणवत्ता और उत्पादन पर मौसम का असर कम हो।
बढ़ने वाला है यूपी का तापमान, आम किसान रहें सतर्क
उत्तर प्रदेश में मानसून की दस्तक के साथ किसानों को राहत की उम्मीद जरूर जगी है, लेकिन मौसम अभी पूरी तरह अनुकूल नहीं हुआ है। उत्तर प्रदेश कृषि अनुसंधान परिषद (UPCAR) के ताजा पूर्वानुमान के अनुसार अगले दो सप्ताह तक राज्य के कई हिस्सों में तापमान सामान्य से अधिक बना रह सकता है।
पश्चिमी और मध्य उत्तर प्रदेश के कुछ क्षेत्रों में अधिकतम तापमान 40 से 42 डिग्री सेल्सियस, जबकि कुछ स्थानों पर 44 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंचने की संभावना जताई गई है। ऐसे में आम के बागों पर गर्मी और बदलते मौसम का दोहरा दबाव पड़ सकता है।
तेज गर्मी, बढ़ती उमस और मानसून पूर्व की छिटपुट बारिश आम के फलों की गुणवत्ता, उत्पादन और भंडारण क्षमता को प्रभावित कर सकती है। फलमक्खी, शोल्डर ब्राउनिंग और अन्य कीट-रोगों का खतरा भी बढ़ सकता है।
यही वजह है कि Uttar Pradesh Council of Agricultural Research ने आम उत्पादकों को बागों की नियमित निगरानी करने, समय पर तुड़ाई करने और मौसम आधारित वैज्ञानिक सलाह अपनाने की को कहा है, ताकि संभावित नुकसान को कम किया जा सके।
क्रॉप वेदर वाॉच ग्रुप की हालिया रिपोर्ट के अनुसार अत्यधिक तापमान और अचानक होने वाली बारिश आम के फलों में कई समस्याएं पैदा कर सकती है। अधिक गर्मी के कारण फलों में नमी की कमी हो सकती है, जिससे उनका आकार और गुणवत्ता प्रभावित होती है। वहीं बारिश और बढ़ी हुई नमी के कारण फल सड़न, शोल्डर ब्राउनिंग, फलमक्खी और अन्य कीट-रोगों का खतरा बढ़ जाता है। तेज हवाओं और आंधी से फलों के गिरने तथा शाखाओं के टूटने की समस्या भी सामने आ सकती है।
यूपीसीएआर ने सलाह दी है कि आम की तुड़ाई सुबह जल्दी या देर शाम के समय की जाए। दोपहर की तेज गर्मी में तुड़ाई करने से फलों की गुणवत्ता प्रभावित हो सकती है। फलों को हाथ से या विशेष आम तोड़क यंत्र की सहायता से सावधानीपूर्वक तोड़ना चाहिए ताकि उनमें चोट न लगे। तुड़ाई के बाद फलों को सीधे मिट्टी के संपर्क में नहीं आने देना चाहिए।
मौसम में नमी बढ़ने के साथ आम और अमरूद के बागों में फलमक्खी का खतरा बढ़ जाता है। वैज्ञानिकों ने बागवानों को मिथाइल यूजिनॉल और क्यू-ल्योर ट्रैप 20 से 25 ट्रैप प्रति हेक्टेयर की दर से लगाने की सलाह दी है। साथ ही नीम सीड कर्नेल एक्सट्रैक्ट का घोल बनाकर 10 से 15 दिन के अंतराल पर छिड़काव करने की भी सिफारिश की गई है। जमीन पर गिरे और कीटग्रस्त फलों को तुरंत एकत्र कर नष्ट कर देना चाहिए ताकि संक्रमण न फैले।
मानसून पूर्व और शुरुआती बारिश के दौरान आम में शोल्डर ब्राउनिंग रोग का खतरा बढ़ जाता है। यह रोग फलों की बाहरी गुणवत्ता को खराब कर देता है और बाजार मूल्य घटा सकता है। बैठक में वैज्ञानिकों ने सलाह दी है कि फल के पकने से पहले फफूंदनाशकों का छिड़काव किया जाए, ताकि रोग का प्रकोप कम हो सके।
गर्मी और नमी वाले मौसम में आम के बागों में कैटरपिलर (कटर कीट) का हमला भी बढ़ सकता है। यह कीट नई पत्तियों और फलों को नुकसान पहुंचाता है। वैज्ञानिकों ने समय-समय पर बाग की निगरानी करने और आवश्यकता पड़ने पर अनुशंसित कीटनाशकों का प्रयोग करने की सलाह दी है।
जिन किसानों ने अभी तक नए आम या अन्य फलदार पौधों के लिए गड्ढे तैयार नहीं किए हैं, उन्हें यह कार्य जल्द पूरा कर लेना चाहिए। गड्ढों की भराई करते समय ऊपरी उपजाऊ मिट्टी में अच्छी तरह सड़ी हुई गोबर की खाद मिलाने की सलाह दी गई है, जिससे पौधों की शुरुआती वृद्धि बेहतर हो सके।
जून महीने में मौसम तेजी से बदलता है। कभी तेज गर्मी तो कभी अचानक बारिश बागवानी फसलों के लिए चुनौती बन सकती है। ऐसे में मौसम आधारित प्रबंधन, नियमित निगरानी और वैज्ञानिक सलाह का पालन करके आम उत्पादक फसल को होने वाले नुकसान को काफी हद तक कम कर सकते हैं।
पश्चिमी और मध्य उत्तर प्रदेश के कुछ क्षेत्रों में अधिकतम तापमान 40 से 42 डिग्री सेल्सियस, जबकि कुछ स्थानों पर 44 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंचने की संभावना जताई गई है। ऐसे में आम के बागों पर गर्मी और बदलते मौसम का दोहरा दबाव पड़ सकता है।
तेज गर्मी, बढ़ती उमस और मानसून पूर्व की छिटपुट बारिश आम के फलों की गुणवत्ता, उत्पादन और भंडारण क्षमता को प्रभावित कर सकती है। फलमक्खी, शोल्डर ब्राउनिंग और अन्य कीट-रोगों का खतरा भी बढ़ सकता है।
यही वजह है कि Uttar Pradesh Council of Agricultural Research ने आम उत्पादकों को बागों की नियमित निगरानी करने, समय पर तुड़ाई करने और मौसम आधारित वैज्ञानिक सलाह अपनाने की को कहा है, ताकि संभावित नुकसान को कम किया जा सके।
तेज गर्मी और बारिश से आम को क्या नुकसान हो सकता है?
अधिक गर्मी के कारण फलों में हो सकती है नमी की कमी
आम की तुड़ाई सुबह या शाम को ही करें
भरी दोपहर में न करें आमों की तुड़ाई
फलमक्खी का बढ़ सकता है प्रकोप
शोल्डर ब्राउनिंग रोग से ऐसे करें बचाव
समय रहते करें कीटों से बचाव के लिए छिड़काव
कैटरपिलर और कटर कीट से रहें सतर्क
नए बाग लगाने वालों के लिए भी सलाह
बागवानी के लिए चुनौती भरा है या मौसम
